title: Fix Alembic duplicate revision cycle
description: Alembic upgrade fails because migration revisions a1b2c3d4e5f6 and b2c3d4e5f6a7 are duplicated, producing a cyclic revision graph. Make the migration graph linear/acyclic and verify Alembic can resolve heads.
close_reason: Fixed by giving the two new migrations unique revision IDs and chaining them after the previous head.

title: Make Layout and sidebar mobile-responsive
description: Below md breakpoint: sidebar slides off-canvas with toggle in TopBar; padding tightens; ActivityPanel becomes overlay. Above md: keep current behaviour.

title: Modernize index.html (fonts, meta, FOUC)
description: Move Google Fonts from CSS @import to HTML link with preconnect — removes render-blocking @import. Add proper title, description, color-scheme, theme-color, viewport tweaks.

title: Frontend modernization and responsiveness epic
description: Make tripl frontend more responsive, faster, and more modern. Quality gates already green (tsc/eslint/vitest). Focus on initial render, mobile/tablet support, and React 19 perf primitives.

title: Add security headers middleware
description: X-Content-Type-Options=nosniff, X-Frame-Options=DENY, Referrer-Policy=strict-origin-when-cross-origin, Permissions-Policy minimal, optional HSTS when secure cookies enabled.

title: Add rate limit on /auth/login and /auth/register
description: Brute-force protection: in-memory token-bucket per IP+email, fail-open on errors. ~5 attempts/15min on login; ~3/hour on register.

title: Enforce ENCRYPTION_KEY in non-debug
description: Currently empty key silently falls back to storing data-source passwords as plaintext. In non-DEBUG mode, app must refuse to start with empty/invalid key.

title: Harden CORS to configured origins
description: Replace allow_origins=['*'] with configurable list from settings (CORS_ALLOW_ORIGINS env). With credentials enabled, '*' is browser-rejected; production must restrict to known origins.

title: Production-ready hardening epic
description: Make tripl production-ready: harden CORS, enforce encryption key, add rate-limit for auth, security headers, request-id/log middleware, and fix 87 ruff lint errors. Tests + mypy + tsc/eslint are already green; this epic addresses real prod gaps without refactoring giant files.

title: Command palette (Cmd+K) for global navigation
description:
Add a Cmd+K (Ctrl+K) command palette that lets the user jump to any event, event-type, scan, or settings tab by typing its name. Single global keybinding, mounted at App level, no per-page glue.

Scope:
- cmdk-react popover; opens on Cmd+K / Ctrl+K, closes on Esc.
- Three sections: Events / Event Types / Settings & Scans.
- Data pulled from already-cached TanStack queries (eventsApi.list, eventTypesApi.list, scansApi.list) with staleTime=5min so opening the palette never adds network latency.
- Selecting an event navigates to /p/:slug/events/:tab/:eventId (preserves current slug + tab).
- Empty state when no project context: only Settings entries are shown.
design:
One component CommandPalette.tsx in components/. Wired into App.tsx so the listener is global. Reads slug from current URL (useParams via NavLink ref or location state) — when on a project route, scopes Events/EventTypes/Scans to that project. When on /data-sources or /, only global entries.

cmdk-react is small (~5KB) and matches Radix Dialog primitive used elsewhere — no new design tokens needed.

title: EventsPage refactor: useEventsQuery hook + split EventsTable
description:
EventsPage.tsx is still 1412 lines after toolbar + saved-views extraction. Two more cuts make it sustainable:

1) useEventsQuery hook: bundle the useInfiniteQuery + URL-sync filters + EVENT_PAGE_SIZE + pagination logic into a single hook returning {events, total, isLoading, fetchNextPage, hasNextPage, filters, setFilter}. Keeps EventsPage focused on layout.

2) EventsTable component: extract the <Table>, header rows, virtualization, DnD reorder sortable context, and TableBody rendering into pages/events/EventsTable.tsx. Takes events + handlers as props; no business logic.
design:
Hook lives at pages/events/useEventsQuery.ts. It owns: filterEtId, debouncedSearch, filterImplemented, filterTag, filterReviewed[ForQuery], filterArchived[ForQuery], filterSilentDays — and constructs queryKey + queryFn. Returns flat events array + helpers.

Table component takes: events, virtualize hint, columns, signal map, window-metric map, callbacks. Existing tests in EventsPage.test.tsx must pass unchanged — the cut is a pure refactor.

title: Alert simulator UX: template preview + cooldown A/B
description:
Two simulator extensions:

1) Live preview: render each simulated firing through the rule's actual message_template + items_template so the user sees the exact Slack/Telegram text that would have been sent. Reuses existing alert_templates render path; no DB writes.

2) Cooldown A/B: in the Replay dialog, let the user override cooldown_minutes (slider) without saving the rule. Endpoint accepts ?cooldown_minutes_override=N and runs simulate_rule_firings with the override. UI shows side-by-side: 'current K firings' vs 'with override M firings'.
design:
Preview: extend SimulatedRuleFiring with rendered_item (string) and AlertRuleSimulateResponse with rendered_message (string). Build a fake AlertDeliveryItem-shaped dict per firing, feed into _build_item_template_context-equivalent. Reuse the no_autoflush pattern for sparkline/top_movers — same call site.

A/B: simulate_rule signature gets optional cooldown_minutes_override; if set, replace rule.cooldown_minutes for the duration of the call. Frontend renders two columns when override differs from saved value.

title: Schema drift: retention cleanup job + sample values
description:
Two follow-ups to the schema-drift v1:

1) Add a Celery beat job that DELETEs SchemaDrift rows past DRIFT_RETENTION_DAYS (currently we only filter on read — the table grows forever).

2) Capture a sample value when we detect new_field / type_changed drift. The cardinality analyzer already has sample values per column; thread them through _diff_event_type_schema -> _upsert_schema_drifts -> SchemaDrift.sample_value. Surface in drift API and badge popover.
design:
Cleanup: new task tripl.worker.tasks.maintenance.cleanup_schema_drifts on a daily schedule. DELETE WHERE detected_at < now() - 30 days. Small footprint, idempotent.

Sample value: CardinalityResult.sample_values is list[str]; take first non-empty value, truncate at 255 chars. type_changed already records observed/declared types, we just attach an example. missing_field doesn't get a sample (no observed data) — keep null.
close_reason: Added _pick_sample_value helper that threads CardinalityResult.sample_values into _diff_event_type_schema for new_field/type_changed drift (missing_field stays null — no observed data). _upsert_schema_drifts persists sample_value and refreshes it on conflict. Both call sites in metrics.py pass cardinality results defensively via getattr to keep mock-based unit tests working. New tripl.worker.tasks.maintenance.cleanup_schema_drifts task + daily Celery beat schedule prunes SchemaDrift rows past DRIFT_RETENTION_DAYS. UI: EventDriftBadge popover renders 'e.g. <sample>' under each drift entry. Tests: 172 backend (+2), ruff+mypy clean.

title: Anomaly explainability: sparkline + top movers in alert delivery
description:
When an anomaly is delivered to Slack or Telegram, the payload should include (a) a sparkline for the last 24 buckets for the scope and (b) the top 3 movers: breakdown values with the largest deviation from expected, so the recipient can see immediately what changed and why.

Scope:
1. tripl/anomaly_context.py: build_sparkline(points, width=24), top_movers_for_anomaly(session, anomaly, limit=3).
2. Hook into _build_delivery_snapshot: pass sparkline and top_movers into each item snapshot.
3. alert_templates.py: add sparkline and top_movers to ALERT_ITEM_TEMPLATE_VARIABLES, and add them to default item templates for each format.
4. Tests: sparkline rendering, top_movers ranking by abs(z_score), end-to-end delivery snapshot.
design:
Reuse MetricBreakdownAnomaly: it already computes per-(column,value) anomalies. top_movers is a query against metric_breakdown_anomalies for the matching scope and anomaly bucket, ordered by abs(z_score) descending, limited to 3. No extra ClickHouse load.

Sparkline is pure functional: reads EventMetric for the last N buckets for the scope and maps values to 8 Unicode blocks. It is safe for all 4 message formats because the same characters are used.
close_reason: anomaly_context.py: build_sparkline (8-block Unicode, width=24), format_top_movers (signed %, +inf% guard, value truncation), load_top_movers ordered by abs(z_score), and load_recent_metric_points for project_total/event_type/event. Wired through _build_item_template_context -> _build_items_text -> _build_template_context -> _render_delivery_message with optional session+scan_config_id. New vars: sparkline, top_movers, sparkline_line, top_movers_line. Updated default item templates. session.no_autoflush is critical because otherwise SELECT triggers autoflush on the JSON column and loses later in-place payload_snapshot mutations. Tests: 168 backend (+3 explainability), ruff and mypy clean.

title: SchemaDrift model + cardinality compare + drift API + UI badge
description:
Extend the cardinality analyzer: for a known event, compare the current field set with FieldDefinition and write SchemaDrift records. UI: red drift badge on EventRow plus a drill-down popover with examples.

Scope:
1. Alembic migration: schema_drifts(id, event_id, field_name, drift_type, detected_at, sample_value, scan_config_id).
2. SchemaDrift model + Event.schema_drifts relationship.
3. Cardinality analyzer writes drift records inside the metrics pipeline after event generation.
4. drift_type enum: new_field, missing_field, type_changed.
5. Retention: clean up drifts older than 30 days in check_metrics_due or a new task.
6. API: GET /projects/{slug}/events/{event_id}/drifts.
7. EventListItem.drift_count in list responses as an aggregate, not the full list.
8. Frontend: drift badge on EventRow with count; click opens a popover with the latest N records.
design:
Drift detection lives in the metrics pipeline, not a separate task: we already have the cardinality analysis result and the match to event_id, so a second pass is unnecessary. Comparison is current column_names vs FieldDefinition.name for the event type using two set differences.

drift_count in list response is a separate COUNT subquery over schema_drifts with 30d retention. This avoids sending a full list payload for catalogs with 1000+ events.
close_reason: Added schema_drifts table, migration, model, and 30d retention. _diff_event_type_schema detects new_field, missing_field, and type_changed; type_changed only applies to auto-created string/json types, so user-curated enum/number/url/boolean fields are not disturbed. Added a metrics pipeline hook before _ensure_event_type_with_fields. Added GET /event-types/{id}/drifts. EventListItem.drift_count is included through a single-query list_events path. UI adds EventDriftBadge on EventRow with details popover. Tests: drift diff unit test (3 types plus idempotent upsert), all tests green.

title: Alert rule simulate endpoint + UI Replay modal
description:
POST /alert-destinations/{dest_id}/rules/{rule_id}/simulate?days=7 walks MetricAnomaly over the selected window and returns virtual deliveries without writing to the DB. UI: a Replay 7d button next to a rule in ProjectAlertingTab opens a modal with bucket, scope, anomaly delta, and rendered template.

Scope:
1. alerting_service: extract _evaluate_rule(rule, anomaly_iter, dry_run) as one path for the live pipeline and simulator.
2. POST /alert-destinations/{dest_id}/rules/{rule_id}/simulate endpoint.
3. Pydantic SimulatedDeliveryResponse: bucket, scope_type, scope_ref, anomaly_id, actual_count, expected_count, z_score, rendered_message.
4. Frontend: Replay button in ProjectAlertingTab -> modal list + warning when the rule is too noisy (>50 matches in the window).
design: Existing pipeline matching is: pull anomalies, check rule filters, gate through cooldown, enqueue delivery. Extract a pure simulate_rule(session, rule, days) function that returns list[SimulatedDelivery]. Cooldown is simulated in memory with a sorted pass and last_fired_at. The template is rendered through the same alert_templates.py code, which guarantees preview equals real delivery.
close_reason: alerting_matching.py: pure rule_matches_anomaly plus simulate_rule_firings with in-memory cooldown and per-scope partitioning. The metrics worker uses the same path. Added POST /alert-destinations/{id}/rules/{id}/simulate?days=N and Pydantic schemas. UI: History button on each rule plus RuleReplayDialog with firings table and noisy warning. Tests: 3 backend tests (matcher cooldown, scope filter, endpoint), all backend (164) and frontend (31) tests green.

title: Event.last_seen_at + Last seen UI column with silent filter
description:
Add Event.last_seen_at (nullable timestamptz). Update it from the metrics pipeline when a bucket has a non-zero count. Show it in EventsPage as a relative-time column and add a Silent > 7d filter.

This is the first atomic step toward the schema drift detector. It is valuable by itself and reduces the risk of one large migration.

Scope:
1. Alembic migration: nullable event.last_seen_at timestamptz.
2. Event model: add the field.
3. Metrics pipeline writer updates last_seen_at = MAX(bucket_ts) when count > 0.
4. Pydantic Event schema: add last_seen_at.
5. EventsPage: new Last seen relative-time column in ColumnsMenu, hidden by default.
6. Toolbar filter: Silent > 7d/30d/never.
7. Backend filter in /events list: silent_since_days query param.
design:
The metrics pipeline already knows event_id and bucket_ts. A single UPDATE in the same transaction as event_metrics insert is enough.

The frontend sends ?silent_since_days=7; the backend translates it to WHERE (last_seen_at IS NULL OR last_seen_at < now() - interval 7 days).
close_reason: Added Event.last_seen_at via migration, bumped it in the metrics writer (monotonic, ignores count=0), added silent_since_days API filtering, and added the Last seen catalog column (hidden by default with bootstrap) plus toolbar select options for Silent > 1d/7d/30d. Tests: 161 backend, 31 frontend, ruff and mypy clean.

title: Split EventsPage.tsx into focused pieces + saved views + command palette
description:
EventsPage.tsx is 1367 lines and currently owns filters, virtualization, metrics, and selection. It is the main product page, and the current structure makes it hard to add features such as saved views, bulk edit, and a sparkline column.

Scope:
1. useEventsQuery hook for filters, sort, pagination, and URL sync.
2. EventsToolbar component for search, statuses, tags, columns menu, and view switcher.
3. EventsTable component for virtualization and DnD reorder.
4. EventsPage.tsx becomes a host component of 300 lines or fewer.
5. Saved views: localStorage model (project_slug -> name -> filter snapshot) and UI to save/load/delete.
6. Command palette (Cmd+K) using cmdk-react for events, event types, scans, and settings tab navigation.
design:
Make the split regression-safe: existing tests in EventsPage.test.tsx should pass unchanged after each refactor step. Saved views are pure client-side first with localStorage; server-side persistence can be added later when auth exists.

Command palette is isolated as one CommandMenu component in App.tsx. It opens with Cmd+K and fetches data through existing query hooks with staleTime=5min.
notes:
Progress 2026-05-15: added pure-client saved views for EventsPage. Current tab + URL search params are stored per project slug in localStorage; toolbar has Views popover to save/apply/delete; storage helper covered by Vitest.

Progress 2026-05-16: translated Beads issue/export text to English and extracted EventsToolbar into frontend/src/pages/events/EventsToolbar.tsx. The page still owns URL-backed filter state, saved-view state, and column visibility callbacks; the toolbar is now a presentational component over those props. Validated with pnpm exec tsc --noEmit, pnpm lint, and pnpm test -- --run.

Remaining scope: extract useEventsQuery, extract EventsTable, add command palette, and continue shrinking EventsPage.tsx toward the host-component target.

title: Alert rule simulator (replay last 7 days)
description:
Alert rules are currently tuned blind: to know whether a rule will fire, the user has to wait for a real anomaly. Add a Replay button directly in the rule form that shows how many times the rule would have fired over the last N days, which events triggered it, and the cooldown effect.

Scope:
1. POST /alert-rules/{id}/simulate?days=7 walks MetricAnomaly over the window, applies rule filters and cooldown, and returns a list of virtual deliveries.
2. Pure function in alerting_service: rule + anomalies -> simulated_deliveries, without writing to DB.
3. UI in ProjectAlertingTab: Replay 7d button next to a rule -> modal list with timestamp, event_type, anomaly delta, and rendered template.
4. Highlight when simulation returns >50 firings in the window with a warning that the rule is too noisy.
design:
Matching logic must be exactly the same as the live pipeline. Extract it into alerting_service._evaluate_rule(rule, anomaly_iter, dry_run: bool) so both the live taskcall and simulate use one path.

Cooldown is simulated in memory: last_fired_at advances through sorted anomaly items.
close_reason: Parent scope is complete via tripl-wtu: shared rule matcher, simulate endpoint, Replay UI modal, noisy warning, and tests are present.

title: Schema drift detector + last-seen in catalog
description:
Event implementation status is currently set manually, and differences between the declared schema and real ClickHouse data are only visible through manual scan-result inspection.

Goal: automatically maintain last-seen for each Event and raise SchemaDrift when known-event data gains new fields or changes dominant type. This becomes the primary signal that the catalog diverged from production.

Scope:
1. Event.last_seen_at (nullable timestamptz) + migration.
2. Metrics pipeline updates last_seen_at for non-zero counts in the window.
3. last seen column in EventsPage + silent > 7d filter.
4. SchemaDrift model (event_id, field_name, drift_type, detected_at, sample_value).
5. Cardinality analyzer compares current fields with FieldDefinition and writes drift records.
6. Anomaly scope=schema flows into the alert pipeline.
7. UI: drift badge on EventRow + drill-down with examples.
design:
Do not duplicate metric tables. last_seen_at is just a projection, updated in the same transaction as event_metrics insert. SchemaDrift is a separate table because it has its own retention; 30 days is enough.

Alert pipeline already knows scope=project/event_type/event; add schema as a fourth scope. alert_templates.py receives variables drift_field, drift_type, and sample_value.
close_reason: Completed remaining parent scope: schema drifts now have an alert scope flag, live delivery/replay candidates, template variables drift_field/drift_type/sample_value/drift_line, UI rule toggle, migration, and tests.

title: Optimistic updates and stable virtual keys for events table
description: placeholder

title: Optimistic updates and stable virtual keys for events table
description: Replace invalidate-then-refetch pattern on toggle/delete mutations with optimistic setQueriesData; add getItemKey to virtualizer so DOM nodes and measurement cache survive cache patches. Big win when the table holds thousands of events: chip clicks no longer trigger 200+row refetches.

title: Adopt React 19 useDeferredValue/useTransition in EventsPage
description: Search input becomes immediately responsive even on large lists via useDeferredValue. Tab switching and filter toggles wrapped in useTransition with isPending indicator.

title: Fix 87 ruff lint errors
description: Run ruff --fix for 51 autofixable issues, then resolve remaining manually (UP042, SIM110, E501, I001). Keep behavior unchanged.

title: Add request-id and structured logging
description: Per-request UUID via middleware, exposed as X-Request-ID header, included in log records. Sets up basic JSON logging baseline for production.

title: Drop legacy CSS classes
description: Remove unused .search-input/.move-btn/.section-divider/.grid-header/.tags-wrap from index.css (grep confirms zero call sites).
